channel.title: The Economist - World in Brief
channel.description: Catch up quickly on the global stories that matter

item.title: World in Brief - February 24, 2026
item.description:
Updated less than 1 hour ago

American stocks climbed in morning trading amid market volatility linked to uncertainty over AI. The S&P 500 rose by more than 0.6% and the tech-heavy NASDAQ by nearly 1%. Fears of AI’s disruption of software firms had caused a heavy sell-off of tech stocks, worsened by Donald Trump’s announcement of a 10% global tariff last week, which came into effect on Tuesday.

European leaders met in Kyiv, Ukraine’s capital, to mark the four-year anniversary of Russia’s full-scale invasion. Ukraine’s president, Volodymyr Zelensky, pressed his guests to give a date for his country’s accession to the European Union, arguing that without one Russia’s leader, Vladimir Putin, would seek to divide the bloc. Hungary recently vetoed a planned €90bn ($106bn) EU support package for Ukraine.
